topics.heading: Fundamentals
points: **Single Responsibility** — Each microservice owns one business capability. Allows independent development, testing, and scaling by separate teams.
**Loose Coupling** — Services interact only through well-defined APIs. Internal implementation changes should never break consumers.
**Independent Deployment** — Each service has its own CI/CD pipeline, release cycle, and versioning. No coordinated deployments needed.
**Decentralized Data** — Each service owns its database (Database per Service pattern). No direct DB access across service boundaries.
**Domain-Driven Design** — Use Bounded Contexts to define service boundaries. Aggregate roots, entities, and value objects model the domain.

topics.heading: Monolith vs Microservices
points: **Monolith** — Single deployable unit. Simple to develop initially, but scaling and team autonomy become bottlenecks as it grows.
**Microservices** — Distributed system of small services. Enables polyglot tech stacks, independent scaling, and team ownership.
**Migration Strategy** — Strangler Fig pattern: incrementally extract services from monolith. Start with least-coupled modules first.
**When to Use Monolith** — Small teams, early-stage products, simple domains. Avoid premature microservices (distributed monolith anti-pattern).
**When to Use Microservices** — Large teams, complex domains, need for independent scaling and deployment. Accept operational complexity trade-off.

topics.heading: Synchronous Communication
points: **REST (HTTP/JSON)** — Most common inter-service communication. Simple, stateless, cacheable. Use proper HTTP methods and status codes.
**gRPC** — High-performance RPC using Protocol Buffers. Binary serialization, HTTP/2, bidirectional streaming. Ideal for internal service-to-service calls.
**OpenFeign** — Declarative REST client for Spring. `@FeignClient(name = "user-service")` with interface methods. Auto load-balanced via Spring Cloud.
**WebClient** — Non-blocking, reactive HTTP client from Spring WebFlux. Supports streaming. Preferred over RestTemplate (deprecated in Spring 6).
**Drawbacks** — Tight temporal coupling, cascading failures, increased latency. Use async patterns when real-time response is not required.

topics.heading: Asynchronous Communication
points: **Message Queues** — Decouple producer from consumer. RabbitMQ (AMQP), ActiveMQ. Point-to-point or pub/sub. Guarantees delivery with acknowledgments.
**Apache Kafka** — Distributed event streaming platform. Topics, partitions, consumer groups. High throughput, durable, replayable event log.
**Event-Driven Architecture** — Services emit domain events. Other services react asynchronously. Enables loose coupling and eventual consistency.
**CQRS** — Command Query Responsibility Segregation. Separate read and write models. Often paired with Event Sourcing for audit trails.
**Saga Pattern** — Manage distributed transactions across services. Choreography (events) or Orchestration (central coordinator). Compensating transactions on failure.

topics.heading: API Gateway
points: **Purpose** — Single entry point for clients. Handles routing, authentication, rate limiting, SSL termination, and request aggregation.
**Spring Cloud Gateway** — Reactive gateway built on WebFlux. Route predicates, filters (pre/post), circuit breaker integration, request rate limiting.
**Backend for Frontend (BFF)** — Separate gateways per client type (web, mobile, IoT). Each BFF tailors API responses for its client's needs.
**Service Mesh** — Infrastructure layer for service-to-service communication. Istio, Linkerd. Handles mTLS, retries, observability without app code changes.
**API Versioning** — URL path (`/v1/users`), header (`Accept-Version`), or query param. Gateway can route to different service versions.

topics.heading: Fault Tolerance
points: **Circuit Breaker** — Prevents cascading failures. States: Closed (normal), Open (fail-fast), Half-Open (test recovery). Use Resilience4j `@CircuitBreaker`.
**Retry** — Automatically retry failed requests with backoff. `@Retry(name = "myService", fallbackMethod = "fallback")`. Use exponential backoff to avoid thundering herd.
**Timeout** — Set max wait time for external calls. `@TimeLimiter(name = "myService")`. Prevents threads from blocking indefinitely on slow services.
**Bulkhead** — Isolate failures by limiting concurrent calls. Thread pool or semaphore based. Prevents one slow service from exhausting all resources.
**Fallback** — Provide degraded response when service is unavailable. Return cached data, default values, or graceful error messages.

topics.heading: Rate Limiting
points: **Token Bucket** — Tokens added at fixed rate, consumed per request. Allows burst traffic up to bucket capacity. Most common algorithm.
**Sliding Window** — Track request count in a rolling time window. Fixed window has boundary issues; sliding window is more accurate.
**Gateway Rate Limiting** — Apply rate limits at API Gateway level. Spring Cloud Gateway `RequestRateLimiter` filter with Redis backend.
**HTTP 429 Too Many Requests** — Standard response for rate-limited requests. Include `Retry-After` header to tell clients when to retry.
**Per-Client Limiting** — Different rate limits per API key, user tier, or IP. Use Redis sorted sets or in-memory caches for tracking.

topics.heading: Service Discovery
points: **Eureka Server** — Netflix OSS service registry. Services register on startup and send heartbeats. Clients discover services by name.
**Spring Cloud LoadBalancer** — Client-side load balancing. Replaces Netflix Ribbon. Round-robin or random instance selection from registry.
**Consul** — HashiCorp service discovery + config store + health checking. Alternative to Eureka with built-in KV store and multi-datacenter support.
**Service Registration** — `@EnableDiscoveryClient` on service. Auto-registers with configured registry. Use `spring.application.name` for service identity.
**Health Checks** — Registry removes unhealthy instances. Eureka uses heartbeats; Consul uses HTTP/TCP/script checks. Actuator `/health` endpoint.

topics.heading: Configuration Management
points: **Spring Cloud Config Server** — Centralized external configuration. Backed by Git, file system, or Vault. Serves config via REST to client services.
**@RefreshScope** — Refresh bean properties without restart. Trigger via `/actuator/refresh` or Spring Cloud Bus for broadcast refresh across instances.
**HashiCorp Vault** — Secure secrets management. Dynamic secrets, encryption as a service, lease-based credentials. Spring Vault integration.
**Config Profiles** — `application-{profile}.yml` per environment. Activate with `spring.profiles.active`. Config Server serves profile-specific overrides.
**Spring Cloud Bus** — Broadcast config changes to all instances via message broker (RabbitMQ/Kafka). Single refresh propagates everywhere.

topics.heading: Distributed Tracing
points: **Micrometer Tracing** — Vendor-neutral observability facade (replaces Spring Cloud Sleuth). Generates trace and span IDs for request correlation.
**Zipkin** — Distributed tracing system. Collects timing data, visualizes call chains, identifies latency bottlenecks across services.
**OpenTelemetry** — CNCF standard for traces, metrics, and logs. Auto-instrumentation agent. Export to Jaeger, Zipkin, or any OTLP backend.
**Log Correlation** — Include traceId and spanId in log patterns via MDC. `%X{traceId}` in Logback. Enables searching logs across services by trace.
**Span Propagation** — Trace context propagated via HTTP headers (W3C Trace Context or B3). Automatic with Spring instrumentation libraries.

topics.heading: Containerization
points: **Dockerfile** — `FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine` for minimal image. Copy JAR, expose port, set entrypoint. Use `.dockerignore` to exclude files.
**Multi-Stage Build** — Build stage compiles with Maven/Gradle; runtime stage copies only the JAR. Reduces final image size significantly.
**Docker Compose** — Define multi-container setups. Service dependencies, shared networks, volume mounts. Ideal for local development with databases and queues.
**Jib** — Build optimized Docker images without a Dockerfile. Maven/Gradle plugin by Google. Layer caching for fast rebuilds.
**Health & Graceful Shutdown** — `HEALTHCHECK` in Dockerfile. Configure `server.shutdown=graceful` and `spring.lifecycle.timeout-per-shutdown-phase` in Spring Boot.

topics.heading: Kubernetes
points: **Deployment** — Manages ReplicaSets and rolling updates. Define desired state (replicas, image, resources). K8s ensures actual state matches.
**Service** — Stable network endpoint for Pods. ClusterIP (internal), NodePort (external), LoadBalancer (cloud). DNS-based service discovery.
**ConfigMap & Secret** — Externalize configuration. Mount as environment variables or volumes. Secrets are base64-encoded (use Sealed Secrets for encryption).
**HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler)** — Auto-scale pods based on CPU, memory, or custom metrics. `minReplicas`, `maxReplicas`, `targetCPUUtilizationPercentage`.
**Helm** — Kubernetes package manager. Charts define templated manifests. Values files per environment. `helm install`, `helm upgrade --set`.

topics.heading: Observability
points: **Logging (ELK Stack)** — Elasticsearch stores logs, Logstash/Fluentd collects and transforms, Kibana visualizes. Structured JSON logging recommended.
**Metrics (Prometheus + Grafana)** — Prometheus scrapes `/actuator/prometheus` metrics. Grafana dashboards for visualization. Alert rules for anomalies.
**Health Checks** — Actuator `/health` for liveness, `/health/readiness` for readiness probes. Custom health indicators for downstream dependencies.
**Alerting** — Prometheus Alertmanager or Grafana alerts. Define thresholds for error rate, latency p99, pod restarts. Route to Slack, PagerDuty, email.
**SLOs and SLIs** — Define Service Level Objectives (99.9% availability, p99 < 200ms). Track with Service Level Indicators. Error budgets guide risk decisions.
